reason: The statement claims that only the elite are named or have dialogue, with no regular people having either attribute. However, in this Exodus 32 passage, the collective people (עָם) clearly have multiple lines of dialogue: they speak to Aaron saying 'ק֣וּם׀ עֲשֵׂה־ לָ֣נוּ אֱלֹהִ֗ים' (Arise, make us gods) and make other collective statements. While these regular people are not individually named (referred to only as the collective 'עָם'), they definitively have dialogue, which contradicts the statement's claim that 'only the elite are named or have lines.'
